specs.section_path: > 5.2 Embodied SCPN: Cellular, Neural, & Systemic Implementation > 3. Structural Geometry and Morphogenesis (L3):
source_formulae: P0R04398: 4. The Infoton-CISS Bridge: Coupling the Psi-Field to Spin Dynamics

P0R04399: The CISS effect demonstrates that chiral biological molecules (DNA, proteins) act as potent spin filters, generating significant spin currents (J_spin) at physiological temperatures. We propose a direct link between this phenomenon and the informational gauge field (A, the "infoton") derived from the foundational gauge principle. The primary mechanism by which the Psi-field interacts with the L3 substrate is the direct coupling of the infoton field to these biologically generated spin currents.
